Memor ndum 
To 
: File 
Date 
March 6, 1978 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSiON 
Ted Prim 
Subject: 
Coastal Commission Code 
The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts 
from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commis-
sion need not be disclosed. 
The justification for this 
decision was that disclosure under the Code already was very 
broad. 
The Committee thought that this type of jurisdictional 
limitation would be appropriate for Codes in which broad dis-
closure already has been imposed upon the highest officials and 
those lower officials who have much contact with persons outside 
the jurisdiction. 
TP:mfa

sections.question_synthetic: Whether gifts from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commission need to be disclosed under the Coastal Commission Code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts from sources outside the Coastal Commission's jurisdiction need not be disclosed, based on the rationale that disclosure requirements under the Code are already broad and jurisdictional limitations would be appropriate for codes with extensive disclosure already imposed on high-level officials.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether gifts from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commission need to be disclosed under the Coastal Commission Code.

CONCLUSION: The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts from sources outside the Coastal Commission's jurisdiction need not be disclosed, based on the rationale that disclosure requirements under the Code are already broad and jurisdictional limitations would be appropriate for codes with extensive disclosure already imposed on high-level officials.

The justification for this decision was that disclosure under the Code already was very broad. The Committee thought that this type of jurisdictional limitation would be appropriate for Codes in which broad dis- closure already has been imposed upon the highest officials and those lower officials who have much contact with persons outside the jurisdiction.
